scenario: Iris Healthcare runs Camunda 8.8 with 4 Spring Boot services that deploy 9 BPMN diagrams via the @Deployment annotation and process 4800 jobs per day. The team still configures camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled in its YAML and must adopt the documented 8.8 replacement before the next release.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled with camunda.client.deployment.enabled at the unified camunda.client.deployment block in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled with camunda.client.worker.deployment.enabled at the unified camunda.client deployment block in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled with camunda.client.cloud.deployment.enabled at the unified camunda.client deployment block in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled with camunda.client.enabled at the unified camunda.client deployment block in the starter.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe.deployment table maps the legacy enabled key to camunda.client.deployment.enabled in the unified deployment block.

b.text: Incorrect. The worker block holds job worker settings; deployment.enabled migrates to camunda.client.deployment.enabled instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.cloud section holds SaaS connection details, not deployment toggles; the replacement is camunda.client.deployment.enabled.

d.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.enabled toggles the client bean; deployment processing is governed by camunda.client.deployment.enabled in 8.8.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.deployment.enabled migrates to camunda.client.deployment.enabled. The unified deployment block controls whether the @Deployment annotation is processed at startup.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
